scope: The parallel loop resolves every role-and-model combination to a driver at startup and exits with a named, actionable error before any worker spawns; workers dispatch only through the driver-backed RunSlice, and a mid-run terminal driver error halts only its own track (no phase-wide cascade cancel).

test_results.command: go test -count=1 -timeout 300s ./cmd/sworn/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./internal/run/... (spec AC-04 command, fresh cache)
go test -count=1 -timeout 300s ./... (full suite, fresh cache — 45 packages ok, 0 FAIL)
go vet ./internal/run/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./cmd/sworn/... && gofmt -l <every changed .go file> (clean; newline-corruption grep over changed .go files clean)

reachability.evidence: go test -count=1 -v -run TestParallelStartupFailFast ./cmd/sworn/ — PASS. Drives the real `sworn run --parallel` CLI entry point (cmdRun), not a leaf unit (Rule 1): with --escalation-models containing the unregistered prefix 'nope/model-x', cmdRun exits non-zero (`sworn run: sworn run: resolve "nope/model-x" for role "implementer": registry: no driver for prefix "nope/" — registered prefixes: ...`) BEFORE openDefaultDB/supervisor.Open/RunParallel run — proven by a post-run query against .sworn/sworn.db's tracks table returning zero rows (no worker's supervisor.Acquire INSERT ever happened, i.e. literally no worker spawned). Two supporting non-leaf-adjacent proofs: internal/scheduler/worker_test.go:TestRunTrack_TerminalDriverErrorHaltsTrack (a fake driver dispatching successfully twice then failing with a run.IsBlocked-shaped terminal error halts RunTrack immediately — TrackFail, 4th slice never attempted) and internal/run/parallel_test.go:TestRunParallel_FailureCascade extended (T1 fails; its independent same-phase sibling T2 still reaches TrackPass in the same RunParallel call — read from the durable per-run loop log .sworn/logs/<release>/loop.log, since RunParallel's return value only reports failedTracks — while the actual dependent T3, depends_on:[T1], reaches TrackSkipped).

delivered.item: AC-01 (amended, S07 captain-proceed.md pin 1): a true pre-spawn startup sweep in cmd/sworn/run.go's --parallel branch resolves the implementer escalation chain, verifier, and captain through the registry BEFORE openDefaultDB/supervisor.Open/RunParallel; an implementer-, verifier-, or escalation-entry resolution failure exits non-zero naming the model, role, and registered alternatives with zero workers spawned; a captain-leg failure is surfaced as a warning and does not block startup (matching RunSlice's own per-attempt fail-open captain policy).
delivered.evidence: cmd/sworn/run.go (startup-sweep block, before openDefaultDB); TestParallelStartupFailFast (cmd/sworn/run_test.go) — non-zero exit + zero tracks-table rows

delivered.item: AC-02: the retired factory helpers newAgentFromModel/newVerifierFromModel remain deleted (confirmed by grep — S06 already removed them); coverage closed by extending internal/run/imports_test.go's TestNoWireImports to scan cmd/sworn, so no future edit can reintroduce a wire-type-holding construction path there.
delivered.evidence: internal/run/imports_test.go scannedPackages includes "../../cmd/sworn"; go test -run TestNoWireImports ./internal/run/ PASS

delivered.item: AC-03: a mid-run terminal driver error surfaces as Status=error/ErrKind through the scheduler and halts only its own track — no phase-wide cascade cancel for an independent sibling, while the actual dependent is skipped.
delivered.evidence: internal/scheduler/worker_test.go:TestRunTrack_TerminalDriverErrorHaltsTrack (track-local halt, no further slice dispatch); internal/run/parallel_test.go:TestRunParallel_FailureCascade extended (sibling T2 TrackPass survives, dependent T3 TrackSkipped, both asserted from the loop log)

delivered.item: AC-04: go test ./cmd/sworn/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./internal/run/... passes; full go test ./... passes (45 packages).

D1 (Coach ack, captain-proceed.md pin 4): ComposeEscalationModels/ResolveDispatch extracted to internal/run/resolve.go, called from both RunSlice (pure refactor, byte-identical error text/behaviour) and the new startup sweep — 'resolvable at startup' and 'resolvable per-attempt' are structurally the same code path.
delivered.evidence: internal/run/resolve.go; internal/run/slice.go (resolution block replaced with ResolveDispatch call); internal/run/resolve_test.go (unit coverage); TestRunSliceResolutionFailure + TestRunSliceCaptainResolutionFailureDefersAndProceeds (internal/run/capabilities_test.go) pass unedited

delivered.item: Design.md's inaccurate TestRunParallel_FailureCascade coverage claim corrected (Captain review pin 3), and the new AC-03 extension asserts the actual T2/T3 outcomes rather than assuming pre-existing coverage.
delivered.evidence: docs/release/2026-06-28-driver-contract/S07-scheduler-failfast/design.md ("AC-03 gets a new test" section, corrected); internal/run/parallel_test.go TestRunParallel_FailureCascade extension

divergence: Touchpoint expansion beyond spec.json's original touchpoints array (Coach-acknowledged, S07 captain-proceed.md pin 4, 2026-07-10): internal/run/resolve.go (new), internal/run/resolve_test.go (new), internal/run/slice.go (edited), and internal/run/imports_test.go (edited) were not in spec.json's touchpoints list. The design's own justification — resolve.go/slice.go is a pure extraction with no behavioural change, imports_test.go is a pure regression guard over code already empty of the banned pattern — held up under verification: TestRunSliceResolutionFailure and TestRunSliceCaptainResolutionFailureDefersAndProceeds (pre-existing tests asserting on RunSlice's exact error text/behaviour) pass with zero edits after the extraction. The Coach pre-authorised recording this as a design_decisions entry (D1, status.json) plus this proof.json divergence rather than routing a separate /replan-release.

AC-01's spec.json text itself was amended before this slice's implementation began (@e8e8858, forward-merged from release-wt, Captain review pin 1 / S07 captain-proceed.md pin 1) to read the fail-open captain-leg policy explicitly — this was NOT an implementer-session edit; it landed via the replan flow prior to design_review being re-entered. Noted here only so the diff base (start_commit c06c13d, cut after the amendment) is not misread as silently narrowing AC-01 unilaterally.
